description: Machine-readable manifest splitting the Label 309 conformance corpus into vectors that MUST be byte-identical across every conforming implementation versus vectors where only structural (semantic) parity is required. Any pair of conforming implementations (e.g. TypeScript, Python, Rust, Go, native mobile) MUST produce byte-identical output for every entry under `byte_identical`. The `structural_parity_only` entries assert the same outcome (recovered plaintext, accepted/rejected verdict, emitted error code) but are not byte-pinned, because their inputs are non-deterministic (random keypairs/nonces) or their wire form admits writer-dependent variation (JSON whitespace and key ordering).

rules.byte_identical: For these vectors `encode(decode(bytes)) == bytes` and every emitted output byte string MUST match across implementations. A diverging byte is a conformance failure.
rules.structural_parity_only: For these vectors implementations MUST agree on the semantic result (round-trip success, tamper/verdict outcome, emitted error code) but the exact byte encoding is NOT pinned.

byte_identical.notes: Bytewise canonical encoding, externally pinned to RFC 8949. Round-trip identity encode(decode(bytes)) == bytes.
Includes the duplicate-key rejection vector; every implementation MUST reject the same byte sequences with the same outcome.

byte_identical.vector_class: Content hash — SHA-256 / BLAKE2b-256 + dual-hash equivalence

byte_identical.notes: Externally pinned, transcribed verbatim from RFC 5869.

byte_identical.vector_class: HKDF-SHA-256 zero-length salt (RFC 5869 section 2.2 absent salt)
byte_identical.notes: Pins the absent-salt extract (salt is the empty octet string, substituted with HashLen zero bytes) against a non-empty info string, mirroring the slots_mac HKDF call so the construction's zero-length salt is byte-exact rather than library-dependent.

byte_identical.vector_class: Argon2id v1.3 KDF + fixed parameter constants
byte_identical.notes: Byte-identical within Argon2id implementation tolerance (RFC 9106).
Externally pinned AEAD ciphertext + tag.

byte_identical.vector_class: ML-KEM-768 + X25519 (X-Wing) — keygen / encaps / decaps / SHAKE expansion
byte_identical.notes: X-Wing primitive output emitted byte-exact by the reference implementation; 1120-byte ciphertext and 1216-byte public key reproduced from pinned inputs. The deterministic-draft10 file pins the full seed -> pk -> (eseed -> ct, ss) chain copied verbatim from draft-connolly-cfrg-xwing-kem-10 Appendix C.
Sourced from the c2sp/CCTV corpus. Every conforming verifier MUST emit valid:false byte-exact. A verifier returning true is using a non-conformant (cofactored) primitive.

byte_identical.vector_class: COSE_Sign1 — Sig_structure / protected header / build / strict-Ed25519 verify
byte_identical.notes: RFC 9052 encoder mechanics plus the production-form record-level COSE_Sign1 (detached payload, domain-separated to_sign prefix).

byte_identical.vector_class: Wallet COSE_Sign1 (CIP-30) — valid per-wallet signed records
byte_identical.notes: Per-wallet CIP-30 signData COSE_Key + COSE_Sign1 wire form; verifiers MUST accept the inline cose_key path byte-exact.

byte_identical.vector_class: Seed-derive — seed -> Ed25519 + X25519 + X-Wing keys (3 seeds)
byte_identical.notes: HKDF info strings cardano-poe-ed25519-v1 / cardano-poe-x25519-v1 / cardano-poe-mlkem768x25519-v1, L=32. Ed25519/X25519 byte-pinned; X-Wing seed + 1216-byte public key emitted byte-exact by the reference implementation.

byte_identical.vector_class: Seed-derive — recipient bech32 encodings (age X25519 + age1pqc X-Wing)
byte_identical.notes: Sealed-PoE recipient public-key encodings; bech32 strings MUST match byte-exact. recipients-from-seed keys the encodings by identity seed; recipient-strings-kat keys them by raw public key (public key <-> exact string, both directions) and pins the HRP / visible-prefix distinction: HRP 'age' renders 'age1...', HRP 'age1pqc' renders 'age1pqc1...'.

Per-slot epk + wrap, slots_mac, ciphertext for pinned inputs. slots_mac = HMAC-SHA-256(HKDF(CEK, info=cardano-poe-slots-mac-v1), slots_hash) where slots_hash = SHA-256(cardano-poe-slots-transcript-v1 || canonicalEncode(SLOTS_TRANSCRIPT)). Content is encrypted under payload_key = HKDF(CEK, salt=nonce, info=cardano-poe-payload-v1) with AAD = canonicalEncode(AD_CONTENT_SLOTS). Per-slot wrap AAD = cardano-poe-kek-v1. The classical epk/wrap are unchanged from the prior corpus; only slots_mac and ciphertext reflect the header-bound construction.

Per-slot kem_ct (reassembles to 1120 B) + wrap, slots_mac, ciphertext. The hybrid KEK is salt-bound: KEK = HKDF(xwing_shared, salt=SHA-256(cardano-poe-xwing-kek-salt-v1 || kem_ct || pub_R), info=cardano-poe-kek-mlkem768x25519-v1), so the wrap binds the reassembled kem_ct and recipient public key. slots_mac and content AAD use the same header-bound construction as the classical path. kem_ct is byte-identical to the prior corpus; wrap, slots_mac, and ciphertext reflect the salt binding.

byte_identical.vector_class: Multi-recipient sealed-PoE unwrap — round-trip + multi-private-key matching + rechunking
byte_identical.notes: Recovered plaintext = original; slot/private-key matching is deterministic given pinned inputs. Hybrid path adds the KEM_CT_LENGTH_MISMATCH pre-decapsulation guard. unwrap-hybrid-rechunked pins kem_ct chunking-invariance: a non-canonical chunk layout reassembles to the same flat bytes and carries the same canonical slots_mac, while a byte-flipped twin fails slots_mac (TAMPERED_HEADER). unwrap-duplicate-recipient pins the legitimate same-recipient-in-two-slots case (fresh distinct ephemerals, same CEK): both slots recover the same CEK, so the CEK-conflict defence does NOT trip and the record decrypts — a verifier must keep decrypting honest recipient padding and reject only when two matching slots recover DIFFERENT CEKs.

CEK = Argon2id(normalize(passphrase), salt, params); payload_key = HKDF(CEK, salt=nonce, info=cardano-poe-payload-passphrase-v1); content AAD = canonicalEncode(AD_CONTENT_PASSPHRASE), which binds the argon2id salt/params and the cardano-poe-pw-norm-v1 normalization profile id (a scheme-fixed constant, never on the wire). Argon2id params are CI-cheap pins below the producer floor. Ciphertext bytes MUST be byte-identical across implementations.

Pins the exact canonicalEncode output of SLOTS_TRANSCRIPT (x25519 + mlkem768x25519), AD_CONTENT_SLOTS (both KEMs), and AD_CONTENT_PASSPHRASE, derived from the committed wrap-n3, wrap-hybrid-n1, and passphrase-n1 inputs. These byte strings are the message hashed into slots_hash and the AEAD AAD, so pinning them catches a cross-SDK canonical-encoding mismatch directly rather than only via a downstream slots_mac / AEAD-tag failure. Every emitted hex byte MUST match across implementations.

Pins kek_salt = SHA-256(cardano-poe-xwing-kek-salt-v1 || kem_ct || pub_R) over the reassembled 1120-byte X-Wing ciphertext and the 1216-byte recipient public key, plus the resulting slots_mac and ciphertext. The salt MUST be byte-identical across implementations.

Tampered slots_mac / wrap / ciphertext MUST be rejected identically across implementations. unwrap-negative additionally pins the header-binding nonce-swap (TAMPERED_HEADER) and the per-slot KEK-reuse rejection (duplicate epk/kem_ct → ENC_SLOTS_DUPLICATE_KEM_MATERIAL).

byte_identical.vector_class: Sealed-PoE construction negatives (header binding / all-zero shared / cross-path)
byte_identical.notes: All-zero X25519 shared secret (the slot is treated as failed; no CEK recovered → WRONG_RECIPIENT_KEY), cross-path confusion (a slots-shaped record decrypted with a passphrase input, or vice versa, MUST be refused before any AEAD), and hybrid header-binding via nonce swap (TAMPERED_HEADER). The rejection verdict is pinned; envelope bytes are deterministic.

byte_identical.vector_class: Record canonical CBOR — maximal record with extension keys
byte_identical.notes: Exercises the full v1 wire surface (items, merkle, enc, sigs, supersedes, tolerated extension keys). encode(decode(bytes)) == bytes.

Fixed-input positive vectors for tree sizes 1,2,3,4,5,7 (RFC 9162 §2.1.1 root construction, §2.1.3.2 inclusion-proof verification). Roots, per-leaf sibling proofs, and the canonical-CBOR leaves-list encodings (with and without the optional leaf_alg key) MUST be byte-identical across implementations. encode(decode(cbor)) == cbor for every leaves-list.
Companion leaves-list schema violations; every implementation MUST reject the same byte sequences with the same emitted error code.

byte_identical.vector_class: Structural validator — negative corpus (one expected code set per Part-A error code)
byte_identical.notes: Each case pins cbor_hex input and the exact expected_error_codes the structural validator MUST emit. Single source of truth for cross-language validator-rejection parity.

structural_parity_only.vector_class: Cross-implementation interop — externally-published sealed record (classical + hybrid)
structural_parity_only.notes: A record published by an independent implementation and decrypted by a separate recipient verifier. Implementations MUST agree on the recovered CEK, the matched item/slot index, and the trial-decrypt verdict; the surrounding fixture envelope is not byte-pinned.
Decrypt(Encrypt(plaintext)) == plaintext and tampered-tag rejection. The fixed-input KAT variants in this same area carry the byte-pinned guarantee.
Round-trip agreement of derived shared secret / signature verification. Deterministic byte-equality is covered by the fixed-vector KATs in the same areas.

structural_parity_only.vector_class: Wallet COSE_Sign1 (CIP-30) — rejection fixtures (tampered / missing / wrong-network)
structural_parity_only.notes: Every verifier MUST reject these with the same verdict (tamper / missing address binding / wrong-network header). The rejection outcome is pinned; the underlying bytes vary per wallet.
Out-of-range seed lengths MUST yield INVALID_SEED_LENGTH across implementations.
